description: Fifth cycle of autonomous improvements. Focus on bulk citation export for selected papers, inline reading status editor, custom user-defined tags, and writing page markdown rendering.

userStories.title: Add bulk citation export for selected papers
acceptanceCriteria: Bulk export button in PapersToolbar for selected papers
Supports BibTeX, APA, and MLA formats
Copies all citations to clipboard as a single block
Works with cross-page selection (select all filtered)
userStories.notes: Reuse the existing toBibTeX/toAPA/toMLA formatters from bibliography-export.ts. The ExportDialog already exists but only exports current page — need to fetch all selected paper IDs or use the effectiveSelectedIds.

userStories.title: Add inline reading status editor in paper row
acceptanceCriteria: Click on reading status badge opens a dropdown with status options
Status changes with optimistic UI update
Status reflects current reading state (unread, reading, read, archived)
i18n labels for all status options
userStories.notes: The reading_status column in papers-columns.tsx is display-only (static badge). Use a Radix DropdownMenu triggered by the badge, similar to how the rating stars work. Use useToastMutation for optimistic update.

userStories.title: Add custom user-defined tags with bulk tagging
acceptanceCriteria: Free-text tag input in InlineTagEditor for custom tags (not just 5 quality presets)
Tag filter dropdown shows both quality presets and recently used custom tags
"Bulk tag operation via 'Add tags' in PapersToolbar for selected papers"
Tags displayed as color-coded badges in paper row
userStories.notes: The Paper type has both tags (free-form) and quality_tags (preset) fields. Currently only quality_tags is used in the UI. Reuse the existing tag mutation pattern. The backend libraryApi.autoTag and collectionsApi.suggestTags endpoints exist but are not wired up.

userStories.title: Render writing page outputs as markdown instead of raw text
acceptanceCriteria: Summarize, Cite, Outline, and Gap Analysis outputs rendered as formatted markdown
Review generation output remains as-is (already handled)
Uses the same MarkdownPreview component from PDF reader
Copy and download buttons still work with raw text
userStories.notes: WritingPage.tsx renders non-review tab outputs in a raw <pre> element. The MarkdownPreview component (lazy-loaded in NotesPanel) can be reused. The copy/download functionality operates on raw text, which should remain accessible.

userStories.title: Add reading streak visualization to analytics
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar heatmap showing reading activity over last 90 days
Streak counter shows consecutive days with reading activity
Click on a day shows papers read that day
Responsive layout for mobile screens
userStories.notes: The backend already tracks reading_streak_days and read_by_week in reading analytics. Need a new endpoint or enhanced analytics response for per-day reading data. Consider using react-calendar-heatmap or a custom SVG grid.
